sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Removed all 4 bars on row 33 (right side). 40 bars remain. Level not yet complete. Bars are removable like level 1.
Plan: Try removing all remaining bars systematically. If the level completes after removing all bars + clicking blue blob, then that's the solution.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Get all remaining gray (w) bars and their center positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
bars = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
print(f"Remaining bars: {len(bars)}")

# Generate click coordinates for each bar
clicks = []
for n in bars:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
    center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
    clicks.append((center_row, center_col))

# Sort by row then col for systematic removal
clicks.sort()
print(f"Click coordinates: {clicks[:10]}... (total {len(clicks)})")

# Batch remove all bars
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in clicks]
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Get all remaining gray (w) bars and their center positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
bars = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
print(f"Remaining bars: {len(bars)}")

# Generate click coordinates for each bar
clicks = []
for n in bars:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
    center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
    clicks.append((center_row, center_col))

# Sort by row then col for systematic removal
clicks.sort()
print(f"Click coordinates: {clicks[:10]}... (total {len(clicks)})")

# Batch remove all bars
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in clicks]
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
